text.title: Internal RFC Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the internal-rfc-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.rationale: Without status field readers cannot tell if the proposal is live.

rule.statement: Body MUST contain `## Open Questions` listing ≥1 unresolved point.
rule.rationale: If everything is resolved it is an ADR, not an RFC.

rule.statement: Body MUST contain `## Alternatives Considered` with ≥2 alternatives.
rule.rationale: Without alternatives the RFC is advocacy, not a proposal.

rule.statement: Frontmatter MUST declare `review_window` (start, end dates) ≥3 working days.
rule.rationale: Indefinite RFCs accumulate and never close.

rule.statement: On status transition to accepted/rejected, the RFC MUST link to the resulting ADR or decision log entry.
rule.rationale: Orphan RFCs erode the decision trail.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
